# Copilot Instructions

## What This Repo Is

A GTD (Getting Things Done) workflow automation system. Bash scripts gather content from an Obsidian vault (`obsidian-vault/Work/`), build prompts from templates in `prompts/`, and invoke `copilot` CLI to produce AI-generated briefings, reviews, and project updates that open in a browser.

The Obsidian vault itself is git-ignored — only the scripts and prompt templates are versioned.

## Running the Workflows

All scripts are run from the repo root:

```bash
./scripts/daily-briefing.sh
./scripts/weekly-review.sh
./scripts/project-sync.sh "NV SCO"
./scripts/gap-finder.sh
./scripts/horizon-scan.sh
./scripts/interview-agent.sh
./scripts/one-on-one-prep.sh
./scripts/process-inbox.sh
```

Set `COPILOT_EFFORT=high` (or `low`) to override the default reasoning effort:

```bash
COPILOT_EFFORT=high ./scripts/weekly-review.sh
```

There are no build steps, test suites, or linters — the "build" is running a script and verifying output opens in the browser.

## Architecture

```
scripts/
  *.sh              # One entry-point script per workflow
  lib/
    common.sh       # Sourced by all scripts — defines log, die, build_prompt, run_prompt
    gather-tasks.sh     # Extracts open tasks from vault by tag
    gather-meetings.sh  # Collects meeting notes from last N days
    gather-project.sh   # Assembles all vault content for one project
    open-in-browser.sh  # Renders markdown → HTML → browser (WSL-aware)

prompts/
  system.md         # Always prepended to every prompt (vault layout, task format, GTD roles)
  *.md              # One task-specific prompt per workflow
```

### Data flow

1. `gather-*.sh` libs pull raw markdown from the vault
2. `build_prompt` (in `common.sh`) prepends `system.md` to the task prompt + gathered context
3. The full prompt is written to a temp file (avoids kernel `ARG_MAX` limits on large contexts)
4. `copilot --allow-all-paths --allow-all-tools` reads the temp file and produces output
5. `open-in-browser.sh` converts markdown output → styled HTML → browser tab

## Key Conventions

### common.sh must be sourced first
Every script starts with:
```bash
source "$(dirname "$0")/lib/common.sh"
```
This sets `PROJECT_ROOT`, `SCRIPTS_DIR`, `VAULT_DIR`, and `PROMPTS_DIR`, and defines `log`, `die`, `build_prompt`, and `run_prompt`.

### Task tags — `#task` is mandatory
A line in the vault is only a real task if it has **both** `#task` and a secondary tag:
- `#task #mine` — I own it
- `#task #follow-up` — assigned to someone else, I'm watching
- `#task #unreviewed` — inbox, needs triage

Lines with only `#mine` (no `#task`) are not action items — AI-generated "Owner:" lines often look like tasks but lack `#task`. `gather-tasks.sh` filters on both tags.

### `Action Items.md` is read-only
This file is auto-generated by the Obsidian Tasks plugin from queries. Never write to it directly. Tasks must be placed in the `## Next Actions` section of a project page or in `Random Notes.md` for inbox capture.

### Project pages have a strict template
Sections (`## Status`, `## Stakeholders`, `## Open Decisions`, `## Blockers`, `## Next Actions`, `## Key Decisions Made`, `## Notes & Context`) must never be removed, even if empty. When updating a project page:
- Always update the `updated:` YAML frontmatter field
- **Open Decisions** and **Blockers** — remove items when resolved
- **Key Decisions Made** — append-only, never remove past entries

### Meeting note filenames determine date filtering
`gather-meetings.sh` parses the date from the first 10 characters of the filename (`YYYY-MM-DD ...`). Meeting notes must be named with this prefix to be included in time-windowed queries.

### Prompt construction pattern
All workflows follow:
```bash
CONTEXT=$(bash "$SCRIPTS_DIR/lib/gather-something.sh" args)
PROMPT=$(build_prompt "task-prompt-file.md" "$CONTEXT")
run_prompt "$PROMPT" "Page Title"
```
`build_prompt` always inserts `system.md` first, so task prompts do not need to re-explain vault layout or task format.

### Vault paths
- `VAULT_DIR` = `obsidian-vault/Work/` (set in `common.sh`)
- Meeting notes live in `Work/Meetings/Notes/`
- Transcripts live in `Work/Meetings/Transcripts/` — skipped by default in gather scripts
- New tasks captured via Obsidian QuickAdd go to `Random Notes.md` with `#unreviewed`
